title: Volatility Navigator EA
subtitle: RSI + Bollinger Bands + ATR Confluence — Dynamic Entry, SL and TP via ATR Multiplier
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — take the ATR-based SL/TP calculation and indicator confluence gate pattern

series_evolution.part_3: EA — live dashboard, auto-refreshes every 2 hours
series_evolution.part_5: EA — first ENTRY-FOCUSED tool. Shifts from analysis/display to actual signal generation with SL/TP. The series pivots here from dashboards to trading engines.

core_concept.name: Three-Indicator Confluence Gate
core_concept.description: Only enter when all three independent indicators confirm the same direction simultaneously. RSI confirms momentum exhaustion (overbought/oversold), Bollinger Bands confirm price at statistical extreme, ATR confirms volatility is sufficient for a meaningful move.
buy_conditions: Close < Lower Bollinger Band  (price at statistical low extreme)
sell_conditions: Close > Upper Bollinger Band  (price at statistical high extreme)
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: This is the conceptual ancestor of our EMA + ADX + VWV confluence gate. The principle is identical: require simultaneous confirmation from momentum, structure, and volatility filters before entering.

atr_multiplier.description: ATR multiplier for SL distance. TP = SL × 2 (2:1 RR).

signal_sound.description: Audio file to play on signal. Replaced in our build by NotificationService.

steps.name: compute_indicators
steps.description: Compute RSI, Bollinger Bands (upper, middle, lower), and ATR on each tick/bar

steps.name: evaluate_buy_signal
steps.description: Check all three buy conditions simultaneously

steps.name: evaluate_sell_signal
steps.description: Check all three sell conditions simultaneously

steps.name: calculate_levels
steps.description: Compute entry, SL, and TP from current close and ATR

steps.name: draw_levels
steps.description: Draw three horizontal lines on chart: entry (green), SL (red), TP (blue)

steps.name: alert
steps.description: Play audio alert and optionally print to console

sl_tp_calculation.critical_note: This is the key insight for CreviaDeriv. The ATR-based SL/TP pattern is the RIGHT approach for dynamic risk sizing.
sl_tp_calculation.weakness: Fixed 2:1 RR ignores zone structure — our system improves by targeting opposing zones for P1 and swing levels for P2/P3

creviaderiv_comparison.similarity: ATR floor for minimum SL distance — we already have this in _calculate_precision_sl() with min 2×ATR floor
creviaderiv_comparison.difference_1: We use structural swings (external HL/LH) as SL anchor, not raw ATR. ATR is only the FLOOR.
creviaderiv_comparison.difference_2: We have 4 TP targets (P1=opposing zone, P2=swing, P3=leg×1.5, P4=2:1 RR). This tool has only P4 equivalent.
creviaderiv_comparison.takeaway: Our approach is strictly superior. This tool's ATR multiplier concept validates our 2×ATR floor design.

outputs.text_label: Buy Signal / Sell Signal at entry level

components_to_build.name: RSICalculator
components_to_build.note: Pure function. Period 14 default. Returns series, caller takes last value.

components_to_build.name: BollingerBandsCalculator
components_to_build.note: Upper = SMA + (StdDev × deviation). Lower = SMA - (StdDev × deviation).

components_to_build.name: SignalEngine
components_to_build.contract: evaluate(rsi, bb_upper, bb_lower, atr, close, config) -> Signal | None
components_to_build.note: Stateless. Called every bar. Returns Signal(type, entry, sl, tp) or None.

components_to_build.name: LevelDrawer
components_to_build.note: Shared across many future parts. Entry=green, SL=red, TP=blue convention.

keep: Three-indicator confluence gate pattern — exactly right architecture for entry filters
ATR-based SL/TP calculation — dynamic risk sizing, not fixed pip values
2:1 minimum RR constraint — we use MIN_RRR=1.5, similar philosophy
Separation of signal generation from trade execution — signals only, human/system decides to act
Green/Red/Blue colour convention for Entry/SL/TP lines
discard: RSI + BB as primary entry triggers — in CreviaDeriv, zones (supply/demand) are the trigger, not RSI
Fixed ATR×1.5 SL — we anchor to structural swings, use ATR only as floor
Fixed 2:1 TP — we use 4 TP targets based on zone and swing structure
MQL5 indicator handles (iRSI, iBands, iATR) — replaced by Python TA-Lib or pandas-ta
ObjectCreate chart drawing — replaced by ChartRenderer / matplotlib in our build
improve: Replace RSI+BB entry trigger with zone interaction (supply/demand zone touch)
Replace ATR SL with structural SL (nearest external swing), ATR as floor only
Replace single 2:1 TP with multi-target system (P1=opposing zone, P2=swing, P3, P4)
Add ATR threshold properly: compare ATR to symbol-specific minimum, not a hardcoded value
Add timeframe filter: require M15 structure alignment before taking M1 entry
Add volume/VWV confirmation — this tool has no volume gate
Add position management: trailing SL after P1 hit, partial close on each target

creviaderiv_insights.sl_calculation_validation: This article confirms the ATR-based SL approach is standard practice. Our improvement: structural anchor + ATR floor is strictly better than raw ATR multiplier.
creviaderiv_insights.rr_ratio_validation: 2:1 RR as minimum is standard. Our MIN_RRR=1.5 is even more permissive — correct given our structural TP targets often exceed 2:1 naturally.
creviaderiv_insights.indicator_confluence_validation: Requiring 3 simultaneous confirmations before entry is the right philosophy. Our EMA3 crossover + ADX rising + departure is the same pattern, just with better-fitted indicators for structure-based trading.
creviaderiv_insights.atr_for_stop_loss: Formula: SL = entry ± (ATR × 1.5). This maps to our ATR floor: min_sl = 2 × ATR. The multiplier differs but the concept is identical — prevent stops being placed in noise.

article_figures.title: Bollinger Bands Visualization
article_figures.insight: Shows price touching lower band — the setup for a buy signal. Price at 2-standard-deviation extreme.

article_figures.title: RSI Formula
article_figures.insight: RSI = 100 - (100 / (1 + RS)). RS = Average Gain / Average Loss over period.

article_figures.title: RSI Chart Example
article_figures.insight: RSI below 30 = oversold zone. Used as momentum confirmation gate for buy entry.

article_figures.title: ATR Formula
article_figures.insight: ATR = max(High-Low, |High-PrevClose|, |Low-PrevClose|). The TRUE range handles gaps correctly.

article_figures.title: Signal Generation Diagram (Crash 900)
article_figures.insight: Shows all three lines drawn on chart after signal. Entry (green), SL (red), TP (blue). Visually clean.

article_figures.title: Visual Alerts Example
article_figures.insight: Alert popup and sound trigger on signal. Shows the notification pathway.

article_figures.title: Volatility 500 Test Results
article_figures.insight: Static result showing signal levels on Volatility 500 index. Signals appear on correct extremes.

article_figures.title: Animated Test Result (GIF)
article_figures.insight: Shows real-time signal generation and price approaching TP level. Confirms dynamic ATR sizing works across different volatility regimes.
